words.definition: Present in large quantities; more than enough
words.example: The garden had abundant flowers this spring.

words.definition: To successfully complete a task or goal
words.example: She worked hard to accomplish her dream.

words.definition: Correct and precise, without errors
words.example: The weather forecast was surprisingly accurate.

words.definition: To obtain or gain something
words.example: He managed to acquire a rare painting.

words.definition: To change in order to fit a new situation
words.example: Animals must adapt to survive in the wild.

words.definition: Satisfactory or acceptable in quality or quantity
words.example: The shelter was adequate but not luxurious.

words.definition: To regard with respect, pleasure, or satisfaction
words.example: I admire her dedication to her work.

words.definition: To publicly support or recommend a cause
words.example: She advocates for better public education.

words.definition: Having a strong desire to succeed
words.example: He was ambitious and worked long hours.

words.definition: To examine something in detail to understand it
words.example: Scientists analyze data to find patterns.

words.definition: Feeling worried or nervous about something uncertain
words.example: She felt anxious before the big interview.

words.definition: Suitable or proper in the circumstances
words.example: Formal clothes are appropriate for the meeting.

words.definition: To evaluate or estimate the nature of something
words.example: The doctor will assess your condition.

words.definition: To accept something as true without proof
words.example: Do not assume you know the answer.

words.definition: An advantage or something that improves a situation
words.example: Exercise has many health benefits.

words.definition: Wide in extent; covering many subjects
words.example: She has a broad knowledge of history.

words.definition: Having the ability or qualities to do something
words.example: He is capable of handling the project alone.

words.definition: Something difficult that requires effort to overcome
words.example: Learning a language is a great challenge.

words.definition: To make something less confused and more clearly comprehensible
words.example: Could you clarify what you mean by that?

words.definition: To work jointly with others on an activity
words.example: The teams collaborated to finish the project.

words.definition: To dedicate yourself to something or pledge to do it
words.example: She committed to learning English every day.

words.definition: Consisting of many interconnected parts; complicated
words.example: The human brain is a complex organ.

words.definition: Giving information clearly using only a few words
words.example: A good essay should be clear and concise.

words.definition: Feeling certain about your abilities or a situation
words.example: She felt confident before the presentation.

words.definition: Acting the same way over time; not changing
words.example: Being consistent is key to building habits.

words.definition: To give something to a shared goal or result
words.example: Everyone can contribute to making the world better.

words.definition: Extremely important or necessary
words.example: Water is crucial for all living things.

words.definition: Eager to know or learn something
words.example: Children are naturally curious about the world.

words.definition: A formal discussion on a topic with opposing views
words.example: There was a heated debate about climate change.

words.definition: To devote time and effort to a purpose
words.example: She dedicated years to mastering the piano.

words.definition: To show clearly by giving evidence or example
words.example: She demonstrated the correct technique.

words.definition: To discover the facts or cause of something
words.example: Scientists worked hard to determine the cause.

words.definition: Showing a great deal of variety; very different
words.example: The city has a diverse population.

words.definition: Achieving maximum productivity with minimum wasted effort
words.example: Electric cars are more efficient than petrol ones.

words.definition: To add more detail or explanation to something
words.example: Can you elaborate on your idea?

words.definition: To become known or come out into view
words.example: New technologies continue to emerge every year.

words.definition: To give special importance or attention to something
words.example: The teacher emphasized the importance of reading.

words.definition: Very large in size or quantity
words.example: The elephant was enormous compared to the dog.

words.definition: Absolutely necessary; extremely important
words.example: Sleep is essential for good health.

words.definition: To set up or found something permanently
words.example: They established a new company last year.

words.definition: To form an idea of the amount or value of something
words.example: We need to evaluate the risks carefully.

words.definition: Plain or obvious; clearly seen or understood
words.example: It was evident she had not slept well.

words.definition: To develop gradually over time
words.example: Languages evolve over hundreds of years.

words.definition: Unusually good; outstanding
words.example: Her performance was truly exceptional.

words.definition: To travel through an unfamiliar area or investigate something
words.example: We love to explore new cities on vacation.

words.definition: Able to change or be changed easily to fit situations
words.example: Her schedule was flexible enough to help.

words.definition: To grow or develop in a healthy way; to thrive
words.example: With proper care, the business will flourish.

words.definition: To concentrate attention or effort on something
words.example: Try to focus on one task at a time.

words.definition: Favored by luck; lucky
words.example: We were fortunate to find a parking spot.

words.definition: To produce or create something
words.example: Solar panels generate clean electricity.

words.definition: Truly what something is said to be; authentic
words.example: Her smile was genuine and warm.

words.definition: Feeling or showing appreciation for something
words.example: I am grateful for all your help.

words.definition: To pick out and emphasize the most important parts
words.example: She highlighted the key points in the report.

words.definition: Having a modest opinion of yourself; not arrogant
words.example: Despite his success, he remained humble.

words.definition: To recognize or establish who or what something is
words.example: Can you identify the main problem here?

words.definition: To put a plan or decision into effect
words.example: They plan to implement the new system next week.

words.definition: To make or become better
words.example: Regular practice will improve your pronunciation.

words.definition: To point out or show; to be a sign of
words.example: The data indicates a rise in temperatures.

words.definition: Certain to happen; unable to be avoided
words.example: Change is inevitable over time.

words.definition: The power to have an effect on people or things
words.example: Music has a strong influence on our mood.

words.definition: Using new ideas or methods; original and creative
words.example: The company is known for its innovative products.

words.definition: To fill someone with the urge or ability to do something
words.example: Great teachers inspire students to think freely.

words.definition: To explain or understand the meaning of something
words.example: It is hard to interpret his body language.

words.definition: To carry out a systematic inquiry to discover facts
words.example: Police are investigating the incident.

words.definition: Following clear and sensible reasoning
words.example: Her argument was logical and well-structured.

words.definition: To keep in good condition; to continue doing something
words.example: It is important to maintain good habits.

words.definition: To reduce something to the smallest possible amount
words.example: We should minimize waste in our daily lives.

words.definition: To give someone a reason or incentive to act
words.example: A good coach knows how to motivate a team.

words.definition: To find your way through a complex situation or place
words.example: She navigated the busy streets with ease.

words.definition: Required to be done; of vital importance
words.example: Sunscreen is necessary when spending time outdoors.

words.definition: Easily perceived or understood; clear
words.example: The answer was obvious to everyone in the room.

words.definition: A set of circumstances that makes it possible to do something
words.example: Learning English opens many opportunities.

words.definition: To succeed in dealing with a problem or difficulty
words.example: She overcame her fear of public speaking.

words.definition: Having or showing strong feelings about something
words.example: He is passionate about environmental issues.

words.definition: To continue despite difficulty or discouragement
words.example: You must persevere if you want to achieve your goals.

words.definition: To cause someone to believe or do something through reasoning
words.example: She persuaded me to try the new restaurant.

words.definition: Having the capacity to develop into something greater
words.example: Every student has the potential to succeed.

words.definition: Marked by exactness and accuracy
words.example: Use precise measurements when baking.

words.definition: To treat something as more important than other things
words.example: You must learn to prioritize your tasks.

words.definition: Very deep or intense; having great importance
words.example: The book had a profound impact on my thinking.

words.definition: Having a sensible and practical idea of what can be achieved
words.example: Set realistic goals to stay motivated.

words.definition: To identify someone or something from previous experience
words.example: I recognized his voice immediately.

words.definition: Closely connected or appropriate to the matter at hand
words.example: Only include relevant information in your report.

words.definition: Worthy of attention; extraordinary
words.example: She made remarkable progress in just one month.

words.definition: Able to recover quickly from difficulties
words.example: Children are often more resilient than adults.

words.definition: Having an obligation to do something; trustworthy
words.example: Be responsible with the resources you have.

words.definition: Important enough to have an effect; noteworthy
words.example: There was a significant improvement in her grades.

words.definition: A plan of action designed to achieve a goal
words.example: We need a clear strategy for the new campaign.

words.definition: To make forceful efforts against difficulties
words.example: Many people struggle with public speaking.

words.definition: Enough; adequate for the purpose
words.example: Is there sufficient food for everyone?

words.definition: Able to be maintained without depleting natural resources
words.example: The company switched to sustainable packaging.

words.definition: Easy to perceive or detect; honest and open
words.example: Good leaders should be transparent with their teams.

words.definition: Being the only one of its kind; unlike anything else
words.example: Each person has a unique set of skills.

words.definition: To make practical and effective use of something
words.example: We should utilize all available resources.

words.definition: Worth a great deal; highly useful or important
words.example: Time is the most valuable resource we have.

words.definition: Very great in size, amount, or extent
words.example: The ocean is vast and full of mysteries.

words.definition: Able to adapt to many different functions or activities
words.example: A versatile employee is an asset to any company.

words.definition: The ability to think about the future with imagination
words.example: She had the vision to build a successful startup.

words.definition: Exposed to the possibility of being harmed or attacked
words.example: Young children are vulnerable to illness.

words.definition: Worth the time, money, or effort spent on it
words.example: Learning a new skill is always worthwhile.
